segments.text: Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense which I now make to you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: When they heard that he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, they were even more quiet. He said,

I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, instructed according to the strict tradition of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God, even as you all are today.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: as also the high priest and all the council of the elders testify, from whom also I received letters to the brothers, and traveled to Damascus to bring them also who were there to Jerusalem in bonds to be punished.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: “As I made my journey and came close to Damascus, about noon suddenly a great light shone around me from the sky.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me,‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ He said to me,‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you persecute.’
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: “Those who were with me indeed saw the light and were afraid, but they didn’t understand the voice of him who spoke to me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me,‘Arise, and go into Damascus. There you will be told about all things which are appointed for you to do.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When I couldn’t see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of those who were with me, I came into Damascus.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: “One Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well reported of by all the Jews who lived in Damascus,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: came to me, and standing by me said to me, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight!’ In that very hour I looked up at him.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: He said, ‘The God of our fathers has appointed you to know his will, and to see the Righteous One, and to hear a voice from his mouth.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: For you will be a witness for him to all men of what you have seen and heard.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: Now why do you wait? Arise, be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord.’
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: “When I had returned to Jerusalem and while I prayed in the temple, I fell into a trance
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: and saw him saying to me,‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not receive testimony concerning me from you.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue those who believed in you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: When the blood of Stephen, your witness, was shed, I also was standing by, consenting to his death, and guarding the cloaks of those who killed him.’
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: “He said to me,‘Depart, for I will send you out far from here to the Gentiles.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They listened to him until he said that; then they lifted up their voice and said,
Rid the earth of this fellow, for he isn’t fit to live!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As they cried out, threw off their cloaks, and threw dust into the air,
the commanding officer commanded him to be brought into the barracks, ordering him to be examined by scourging, that he might know for what crime they shouted against him like that.
When they had tied him up with straps, Paul asked the centurion who stood by,
Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman, and not found guilty?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: When the centurion heard it, he went to the commanding officer and told him,
Watch what you are about to do, for this man is a Roman!
segments.speaker: Centurion

segments.text: The commanding officer came and asked him,
Tell me, are you a Roman?
segments.speaker: The commanding officer came and

segments.text: He said,
Yes.
segments.speaker: The commanding officer came and

segments.text: The commanding officer answered,
I bought my citizenship for a great price.
segments.speaker: The commanding officer

segments.text: Paul said,
But I was born a Roman.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Immediately those who were about to examine him departed from him, and the commanding officer also was afraid when he realized that he was a Roman, because he had bound him.

But on the next day, desiring to know the truth about why he was accused by the Jews, he freed him from the bonds and commanded the chief priests and all the council to come together, and brought Paul down and set him before them.
